questions.question: In `<script setup>`, how does Vue recognize a variable as a custom directive?
options: It must be registered with `app.directive()` before the component mounts
It must be exported from a `.directive.ts` file
Any variable starting with `v` followed by an uppercase letter is automatically treated as a directive
It must be declared inside a `directives: {}` block within the setup function

questions.question: What information does the `binding.arg` property contain?
options: The current value passed to the directive
The argument after the colon in `v-directive:arg`
The list of modifier flags as an object
The previous value from the last update cycle

questions.question: Why must side effects like ResizeObserver be cleaned up in the `unmounted` hook?
options: Vue requires it to pass TypeScript type checks
To prevent memory leaks when the element is removed from the DOM
Because observers are automatically paused but not stopped on hide
To allow the directive to be re-used on another element without conflict
